name: Rate Limiting & Load Shedding
def: Capping request rate (rate limiting) and dropping excess work under overload (load shedding).
problem: Protect a service from overload, abuse and cascading failure when demand exceeds capacity.
mechanism: Token-bucket/leaky-bucket meters allow N requests per interval; beyond capacity, low-priority requests are shed (rejected fast) to keep the system healthy.
limits: Rejecting legitimate traffic at the limit
Distributed rate limiting is hard (shared counters)
whenNot: Under-utilised internal systems with no abuse risk
myths: "Rate limiting and load shedding are the same" — one caps rate, the other drops under stress
